title: rhinanthus crista-galli subsp. stenophyllus
intro: Rhinanthus crista-galli subsp. stenophyllus, also known as Stenophylla, is a subspecies of the common broomrape, a hemiparasitic plant that obtains some of its nutrients by connecting to the roots of other plants. The medicinal properties of Stenophylla include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve digestion, while also acting as an antiseptic and a diuretic. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, and its potential to support the immune system. Stenophylla can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
